title: std::map - looking values up by key
author: Prodipta Acharjee
concept: std::map stores key-value pairs and lets you fetch a value by its key with [ ]. (It also keeps the keys sorted.)
explanation: The STL hands you the containers you'd otherwise build by hand. std::map is the dictionary: ages["min"] = 20 stores it, ages["min"] reads it back. Indexing a key that isn't there quietly creates it. A std::map keeps its keys in sorted order (lookup is O(log n)); its cousin std::unordered_map drops the ordering for average O(1). Either way, it's lookup by name instead of by position.

exercises.question: How do you store or read a value in a std::map?
